codeReview.summary: F017-BE requirements are fully satisfied by pre-existing code that was verified in this session. The commit itself contains no new source code for F017-BE (only unrelated validation artifacts from other milestones), but the codebase already has: (1) ConflictResolutionDialog.tsx with local/remote comparison, merge editor, and resolution buttons (Use Local, Use Remote, Merge Manual, Discard), (2) SyncStatusBar.tsx with a red conflict-count badge that opens the dialog on click and refreshes after resolution, (3) syncDatabase.ts functions getPendingConflicts, resolveConflict, discardConflict with full audit trail, (4) main.ts IPC handlers under the sync:* namespace, and (5) syncStatusTracking.ts broadcasting real-time updates via sync:transactionStatusChanged. All validators pass (typecheck, lint, 218 tests).

issues.description: The onResolved callback unconditionally sets pendingConflictsCount to 0. While ConflictResolutionDialog only calls this after the final conflict is resolved (so it appears to work), the callback is semantically per-resolution. If the dialog behavior ever changes to fire onResolved after each individual resolution, the badge would incorrectly show 0 while conflicts remain until the next 30-second interval refresh.

The pending-conflicts count refreshes on a 30-second polling interval but there is no IPC listener for sync:transactionStatusChanged (or a dedicated conflict-detected event) to update the badge immediately when a new conflict is detected. This can cause the badge to lag up to 30 seconds after a conflict appears.

No new tests were added in this session. The handoff correctly notes that the feature was already implemented in prior milestones and existing tests cover it, but there are no dedicated unit/integration tests for the conflict-resolution IPC handlers (sync:getPendingConflicts, sync:resolveConflict, sync:discardConflict).

sharedStateObservations.observation: backend-worker SKILL.md assumes all features require new implementation and mandates TDD (write tests first). It does not provide guidance for verification-only tasks where the code already exists from a previous milestone. The worker handled this appropriately by treating it as verification, but the skill could benefit from a 'Verify Existing Implementation' procedure step.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows the worker discovered the code was already implemented, ran validators, and committed a verification commit with no new source-code changes. SKILL.md Work Procedure steps 3-5 assume net-new files/handers will be created.

summary: F017-BE passes review. The Conflict Resolution Dialog Integration is fully implemented across frontend (ConflictResolutionDialog, SyncStatusBar), backend (syncDatabase IPC handlers), and real-time status broadcasting (syncStatusTracking). The commit for this feature was a verification commit with no new source changes because the implementation already existed and works correctly. Validators all pass. Minor non-blocking gaps: SyncStatusBar resets conflict count to 0 blindly in onResolved, and conflict badge updates are poll-based (30s) rather than event-driven. No blocking issues.
